criticalExhaustiveGaps.label: Local providers, clinics, therapists, and care
criticalExhaustiveGaps.evidence: 83 public provider rows and 23 staged provider rows across all 50 states.
criticalExhaustiveGaps.exhaustiveGap: This is the biggest information hole if the goal is all local help, especially to compete on care and treatment discovery.

criticalExhaustiveGaps.label: Advocates and legal/IEP support
criticalExhaustiveGaps.evidence: 2995 advocate rows, with California still blocked in strict gold because 58 counties lose advocate coverage after truth gating.
criticalExhaustiveGaps.exhaustiveGap: Advocate count is decent, but truth-safe local coverage is not yet strong enough to call exhaustive.

criticalExhaustiveGaps.label: Findhelp-like metadata: availability, accessibility, intake, capacity
criticalExhaustiveGaps.evidence: Provider accessibility booleans with true signal: accepts_medi_cal 27/83; interpreter_available 22/83; asl_available 12/83; wheelchair_accessible 5/83; virtual_services 18/83; in_person_services 60/83; home_visits 3/83; transportation_help 8/83 Nonprofit accessibility booleans with true signal: interpreter_available 0/29499; asl_available 0/29499; wheelchair_accessible 0/29499; virtual_services 1/29499; in_person_services 22/29499; home_visits 0/29499; transportation_help 0/29499 Advocate accessibility booleans with true signal: interpreter_available 0/2995; asl_available 0/2995; wheelchair_accessible 0/2995; virtual_services 2/2995; in_person_services 0/2995; home_visits 0/2995; transportation_help 0/2995
criticalExhaustiveGaps.exhaustiveGap: The schema exists, but live high-signal metadata is sparse, especially on nonprofits and advocates.

criticalExhaustiveGaps.label: Canonical org -> program -> location normalization
criticalExhaustiveGaps.evidence: 36891 organizations, 36891 org-program links, 83 service locations, 4314 office locations, 33130 virtual service areas.
criticalExhaustiveGaps.exhaustiveGap: Normalization exists, but service-location depth is still too thin to support a fully location-rich help product.

criticalExhaustiveGaps.label: Conditions, functional needs, age bands, insurance types
criticalExhaustiveGaps.evidence: 78 conditions, 18 functional needs, 0 age bands, 0 insurance types.
criticalExhaustiveGaps.exhaustiveGap: Conditions and needs are strong, but age-band and insurance reference tables are still empty.

criticalExhaustiveGaps.label: Help content and explanatory knowledge
criticalExhaustiveGaps.exhaustiveGap: The content layer is far too small for a product that aims to cover the full family journey nationally.

criticalExhaustiveGaps.label: Family workflow, case tracking, reminders, and documents
criticalExhaustiveGaps.evidence: 5 total family-case-layer rows across 8 tables; most tables are empty.
criticalExhaustiveGaps.exhaustiveGap: The runtime workflow model exists but is not populated as a real product layer.

criticalExhaustiveGaps.label: Support planning, collaboration, and care coordination
criticalExhaustiveGaps.exhaustiveGap: This entire product surface is still schema-only in practice.

criticalExhaustiveGaps.label: User submissions, coverage gaps, verification queue
criticalExhaustiveGaps.evidence: 0 user submissions, 0 coverage gaps, 2 verification queue items.
criticalExhaustiveGaps.exhaustiveGap: Operational feedback loops are not built out enough to sustain exhaustive maintenance.

families.label: First-party advocate and legal-support sources
families.reason: Current advocate/legal targets are overwhelmingly quarantined COPAA-style directories and need replacement with first-party or official sources.

families.label: Exact forms libraries for most states
families.reason: Only 1 forms source is currently scrape-ready; most state form targets are blocked by fake-domain patterns or still too weak.

families.label: Repair generated fake official domains
families.reason: Many state office, DD, waiver, early intervention, and form targets are blocked because the repo currently has generated fake official domains.

families.label: More named first-party provider targets
families.reason: Provider coverage is still the biggest visible info gap even after the ready provider URLs are scraped.

families.label: Knowledge article source families
families.reason: The product needs many more explanatory content inputs, but the current target inventory is mostly directory/routing oriented.
